description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese shamisen, a three-stringed musical instrument with a rectangular, box-like body covered in a light-colored skin or synthetic material. The body is bordered by a dark wooden frame, and the long, slender neck extends from the body, made of polished wood with a deep brown hue. The headstock is fitted with three large tuning pegs, arranged in a distinctive triangular pattern, and the bridge and tailpiece are visible on the body. The instrument features a combination of smooth, glossy wood and matte, textured skin, with some metallic elements on the tuning pegs. The overall design is elegant and functional, with visible seams and joints typical of hand-crafted string instruments.
description: A traditional Japanese shamisen with a rectangular body, light skin covering, and a long, polished wooden neck with three large tuning pegs.
description_view_0: Front angle showing the rectangular body, skin covering, and the neck with tuning pegs clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the neck and the thin profile of the body.
description_view_2: Top-down view focusing on the headstock, tuning pegs, and the bridge placement on the body.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the back of the body and the full extension of the neck.
attribute_headstock_design: Rectangular headstock with three large tuning pegs arranged in a triangular pattern, no visible logo.
